content.content:
60	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
61	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
62	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
63	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](bugs.md))
64	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
65	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
66	   former Todos live in
67	   [refactor-20260716.md](refactor-20260716.md).
68	   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
69	     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
70	     split push.rs → jj-lib migration → push body-intro
71	     validation → trapezoid close-out → por → dual
72	     conversion.
73	3. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
74	   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
75	   sequence:
76	   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
77	     stages, ochids injected both directions)
78	   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
79	     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
80	   - publish
81	
82	   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
83	   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
84	   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
85	   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
86	   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
87	     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
88	     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
89	   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
90	     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
91	     normally, before the rebase.
92	   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
93	     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
94	     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
95	     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
96	     operation).
97	   - Interim recipe only: the refactor program's
98	     [trapezoid close-out stage](refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out)
99	     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
100	     path until it lands.
101	
102	4. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
103	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
104	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
105	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
106	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
107	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
108	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
109	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
110	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
111	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
112	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
113	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
114	   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
115	   the cycle after 0.65.2.
116	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
117	     not a pre-assigned version?
118	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
119	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
120	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
121	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
122	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
123	   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
124	     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
125	     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
126	     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
127	5. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
128	   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
129	   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
130	   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
131	   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
132	   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
133	     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
134	     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
135	   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
136	   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
137	6. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
138	   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
139	   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
140	   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
141	   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
142	   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
143	   single-mode sync cycle.
144	   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
145	     halts the push before anything is committed); the
146	     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
147	   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
148	     nothing invokes it.
149	   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
150	     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
151	     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).
152	7. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
153	   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
154	   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
155	   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
156	   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
157	   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
158	   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
159	   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
160	     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
161	     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
162	   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
163	     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
164	     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
165	     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
166	     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
167	   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes
168	     files (`todo.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`) so the
169	     no-arg pre-commit run covers them all. Fixed rather than
170	     a `notes/**.md` walk because prose docs
171	     (`cycle-protocol.md`, design notes) carry ordinary
172	     numbered lists that aren't managed sequences — a walk
173	     would false-positive (markdown renders `1. 1. 1.` as
174	     1-2-3, a legitimate prose pattern).
175	   - Override args follow the `--init-from` convention:
176	     positional files/dirs (a dir → its `*.md`) plus an
177	     `@<file>` manifest, additive — for ad-hoc validation of
178	     a specific file.
179	   - Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
180	     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
181	8. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**
182	   The pre-commit (cargo cycle: fmt/clippy/test/install) only
183	   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
184	   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
185	   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). (Since 0.69.0-3 push's
186	   `preflight` no longer re-runs the cargo cycle — vc-x1
187	   assumes nothing about repo contents — so the pre-commit is
188	   the *only* gate, strengthening the no-skip case.)
189	   - Adopt one rule, no exception: the pre-commit runs before
190	     Work review on every commit. (docs: AGENTS.md Cycle
191	     Protocol summary + cycle-protocol.md per-commit-flow.)
192	   - Enrich the pre-commit so it's meaningful on docs commits:
193	     add the doc validators — `validate-numbering` (its own
194	     Todo, a prereq) plus `validate-repo` when it exists.
195	     Whether push's `preflight` may run them needs a decision
196	     against the content-agnostic principle (they read
197	     `notes/` — repo content; the repo-declared-checks idea
198	     was rejected 2026-07-15 in favor of "run checks
199	     yourself").
200	   - This dissolves the docs exception: with doc validators in
201	     the pre-commit there's always something to validate, so
202	     the carve-out stops making sense.
203	   - Its own near-term cycle (chosen over a 0.61.1 insert to
204	     avoid rewriting published 0.62.0-x history); no version
205	     pre-assigned — see the Todo "Version-number protocol is
206	     fragile" on fragile version targets.
207	9. **vc-x1 push: record uncovered code commits (N:1 code↔bot).**
208	   Today push assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared
209	   title/body. The interop / adoption scenario breaks that:
210	   the code side is worked single-repo style (commit +
211	   `jj git push` / `git push`, no `vc-x1 push` in the loop),
212	   so no bot pairings exist — one bot commit then records
213	   every code commit not yet covered by a prior `ochid:`,
214	   via a multi-line `ochid:` per the design in [[10]].
215	   - Also covers a cycle held local and published all at
216	     once (the ochid-trailers section's "one ochid per Work
217	     commit" on merge close-out) — work commits never
218	     individually paired.
219	   - Out of scope: the trapezoid close-out. That flow stays
220	     1:1 (the close-out pair commits normally, then the
221	     merge rebase; chids survive rebase, so ochids stay
222	     valid); its enabler is the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause
223	     point between commit and publish stages".
224	   - Teach push to:
225	     - detect the shape (code WC empty, uncovered commits at
226	       the bookmark)
227	     - skip `commit-app`
228	     - compose a `.claude`-specific message
229	     - emit one `ochid:` line per uncovered commit
230	   - Open: computing "uncovered" — likely a revset from the
231	     code bookmark back to the newest commit referenced by
232	     the bot journal's ochids.
233	10. **Run validate-bot at every vc-x1 invocation
234	    (config-gated).** The check is one jj spawn
235	    (`jj bookmark list main --all-remotes`), cheap enough
236	    to run at every execution — noted 2026-07-15 as a
237	    "could, not should". Design points:
238	    - locate the bot repo (`<cwd>/.claude` or config;
239	      shares the lookup with the refactor program's
240	      [facade-owns-topology stage](refactor-20260716.md#stage-facade-owns-topology))
241	      and silently skip when absent
242	    - severity knob in `.vc-config.toml`
243	      (`warn|error|off`): unrelated commands (fix-todo)
244	      warn at most; push / squash-push / validate-bot
245	      already have their own handling from 0.69.0-3
246	11. **README: audit flag tables and examples against the
247	    current CLI.** 0.69.0-4 fixed the init section (it
248	    documented retired `--owner` / `--dir` / `--repo-local`
249	    flags) and the 0.69.0 surfaces, but the README's other
250	    tables (clone, symlink, sync, revert, list/desc/chid/
251	    show) have never had a systematic `-h` comparison and
252	    drift silently.
253	    - Sweep each section against `vc-x1 <cmd> -h`.
254	    - Consider regenerating transcripts via support
255	      scripts (the gen-exmpl pattern) so examples stay
256	      reproducible.
257	
258	## Ideas
259	
260	 Items not yet solid enough for `## Todo` (or surfaced
261	 during close-out / end-of-day before they're fully
262	 formed). Triaged at the next Preparation: promote to
263	 `## Todo` / `notes/todo-backlog.md`, fold into a
264	 picked-up cycle, or drop.
265	
266	1. **Codify ochid invariant + bot-repo rules + squash
267	   gating + cross-repo migration in
268	   `notes/cycle-protocol.md`.** Was planned for
269	   `0.59.0-2` but deferred when 0.59.0 closed out via
270	   squash + Option F (manual bot-side rewrite). The
271	   rules were exercised manually for the close-out;
272	   this Idea formally codifies them.
273	   - Codify the **ochid invariant** in `## ochid
274	     trailers`: every public ochid must resolve in the
275	     public graph.
276	   - Codify bot-repo rules: never squashed; descriptions
277	     editable (`jj describe` preserves chid).
278	   - Codify squash gating: until `vc-x1 push --squash`
279	     exists, manual symmetric squash (Option F: app
280	     squash + bot-side trailer rewrite + force-push) is
281	     the standard recipe; merge non-ff is the default
282	     shape for multi-commit cycles (the concrete recipe is
283	     its own Todo — "merge-non-ff recipe").
284	   - Sketch cross-repo migration: ochids change at
285	     every merge until the change reaches the canonical
286	     repo's `main`.
287	   - Apply Ideas-aware Preparation/Close-out updates:
288	     Preparation triages `## Ideas` (promote / fold /
289	     drop) before declaring the plan; Close-out
290	     captures unresolved follow-ups into `## Ideas`.
291	
292	2. **`vc` as a code+conversation provenance tool (grander
293	   ambition).** Today `vc-x1` manages a dual repo (code +
294	   `.claude`) cross-linked by `ochid:`. The larger aim is
295	   to *surface* that link — view history with the
296	   conversation and the code side by side: provenance, the
297	   *why* of a change, not just the *what*. The dual-repo +
298	   `ochid` design is already the substrate; the cross-links
299	   make code↔conversation navigable, so the viewer is UI
300	   over an already-solved data link.
301	   - Build direction: keep resolution/assembly in `vc` — an
302	     editor-agnostic Rust engine/lib extending the
303	     `show` / `chid` / `desc` family ("given a commit,
304	     resolve its ochid and assemble the paired diff +
305	     conversation slice"); the editor add-on is a thin
306	     presentation layer over it.
307	   - Front-end leans a Zed add-on (Rust, preferred), maybe
308	     VSCode / other. Verify Zed's extension API can host a
309	     rich side-by-side panel before committing — an
310	     editor-agnostic core hedges the bet.
311	   - `vc-x2`? A rewrite is unwarranted: the audit's
312	     Commonality pass found the architecture sound (por is
313	     bolted on where an existing good pattern wasn't
314	     applied) — equalize incrementally. "vc-x2" only makes
315	     sense if the viewer changes the *core* architecture
316	     (an index / daemon / data model). Separate
317	     engine-rewrite (no) from product-reposition (open).
318	   - Possible artifact: a top-level
319	     `notes/design-cli/vision.md` framing the direction,
320	     with the parity and conversion docs as sub-designs.
321	3. **Restructure the design-cli parity docs (target

323	   fuses a *frozen* audit (the `## 1`–`## 8` snapshot
324	   evidence) with a *living* design (axes, decisions,
325	   matrix, gap list); the "audit" name undersells it and
326	   the halves have different lifecycles. And
327	   `por-dual-parity.md` (the stub) overlaps on parity but
328	   uniquely holds the `por ↔ dual` conversion design.
329	   - Split the audit doc into a frozen audit snapshot + a
330	     living design doc (names TBD; could reclaim
331	     `por-dual-parity.md` for the design).
332	   - Refocus the stub to conversion-only and rename (e.g.
333	     `por-dual-conversion.md`); drop its redundant parity
334	     half.
335	   - Repoint refs (`todo.md` `[1]` + the `por → dual` Todo,
336	     `copying.md`, the audit's internal anchors + Reading
337	     guide) and validate; `chores-10/11/12` mentions are
338	     historical and stay.
339	   - Promote the Gap-list items to anchored
340	     `#### Gap N — <title>` sub-headings so cross-cycle
341	     citations can deep-link a specific gap (markdown
342	     anchors headings, not list items). Trade-off: stable
343	     anchors, but the ordinal lives in the heading text
344	     (manual renumber on reorder) — fine for a consumed
345	     backlog. The 3 `Gap #N` links in the `0.62.0`
346	     close-out chores narrative resolve only to the section
347	     until this lands.
348	   - Deferred from the 0.62.0 close-out: close-out is
349	     bookkeeping-only, and the split is substantive,
350	     anchor-heavy work warranting its own cycle.

352	## Bugs

359	and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

file.content:
mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](bugs.md))
   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
   former Todos live in
   [refactor-20260716.md](refactor-20260716.md).
   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
     split push.rs → jj-lib migration → push body-intro
     validation → trapezoid close-out → por → dual
     conversion.
3. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
   sequence:
   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
     stages, ochids injected both directions)
   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
   - publish

   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
     normally, before the rebase.
   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
     operation).
   - Interim recipe only: the refactor program's
     [trapezoid close-out stage](refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out)
     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
     path until it lands.

4. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
   the cycle after 0.65.2.
   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
     not a pre-assigned version?
     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
       A contributors email address would do it, but also
       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
5. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
6. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
   single-mode sync cycle.
   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
     halts the push before anything is committed); the
     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
     nothing invokes it.
   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to
     push's commit-stage rollback (today it auto-runs
     `jj op restore`, hiding the evidence).
7. **validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all
   sequence-managed notes files generically.** `validate-todo`
   / `fix-todo` only operate on the single file passed, so a
   renumber slip in `bugs.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, or
   `todo.md`'s `## Ideas` section passes unnoticed — too weak
   for a pre-commit gate. Prereq for the pre-commit doc
   validators (Todo "pre-commit: single rule ...").
   - Rename the pair: `validate-todo` → `validate-numbering`,
     `fix-todo` → `fix-numbering` — they validate numbered-
     sequence integrity, not todos specifically.
   - Generic detection: for every `#…#` section, validate the
     column-0 `^\d+\.␠` entries form a contiguous 1..N run.
     Drops the Todo/Bugs special-casing; auto-covers
     `## Ideas` and any new numbered section. Keep the
     column-0 anchor so indented sub-lists aren't counted.
   - Default scope: a fixed list of sequence-managed notes
     files (`todo.md`, `todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`) so the
     no-arg pre-commit run covers them all. Fixed rather than
     a `notes/**.md` walk because prose docs
     (`cycle-protocol.md`, design notes) carry ordinary
     numbered lists that aren't managed sequences — a walk
     would false-positive (markdown renders `1. 1. 1.` as
     1-2-3, a legitimate prose pattern).
   - Override args follow the `--init-from` convention:
     positional files/dirs (a dir → its `*.md`) plus an
     `@<file>` manifest, additive — for ad-hoc validation of
     a specific file.
   - Open: revisit fixed-vs-glob at implementation if the
     fixed list proves annoying to maintain.
8. **pre-commit: single rule (no docs skip) + doc validators.**
   The pre-commit (cargo cycle: fmt/clippy/test/install) only
   checks code, so it's "skip-able for purely-docs commits" —
   but that exception is exactly where checks slip (skipped on
   0.62.0-7/-8 until caught). (Since 0.69.0-3 push's
   `preflight` no longer re-runs the cargo cycle — vc-x1
   assumes nothing about repo contents — so the pre-commit is
   the *only* gate, strengthening the no-skip case.)
   - Adopt one rule, no exception: the pre-commit runs before
     Work review on every commit. (docs: AGENTS.md Cycle
     Protocol summary + cycle-protocol.md per-commit-flow.)
   - Enrich the pre-commit so it's meaningful on docs commits:
     add the doc validators — `validate-numbering` (its own
     Todo, a prereq) plus `validate-repo` when it exists.
     Whether push's `preflight` may run them needs a decision
     against the content-agnostic principle (they read
     `notes/` — repo content; the repo-declared-checks idea
     was rejected 2026-07-15 in favor of "run checks
     yourself").
   - This dissolves the docs exception: with doc validators in
     the pre-commit there's always something to validate, so
     the carve-out stops making sense.
   - Its own near-term cycle (chosen over a 0.61.1 insert to
     avoid rewriting published 0.62.0-x history); no version
     pre-assigned — see the Todo "Version-number protocol is
     fragile" on fragile version targets.
9. **vc-x1 push: record uncovered code commits (N:1 code↔bot).**
   Today push assumes 1:1 symmetric WC commits with shared
   title/body. The interop / adoption scenario breaks that:
   the code side is worked single-repo style (commit +
   `jj git push` / `git push`, no `vc-x1 push` in the loop),
   so no bot pairings exist — one bot commit then records
   every code commit not yet covered by a prior `ochid:`,
   via a multi-line `ochid:` per the design in [[10]].
   - Also covers a cycle held local and published all at
     once (the ochid-trailers section's "one ochid per Work
     commit" on merge close-out) — work commits never
     individually paired.
   - Out of scope: the trapezoid close-out. That flow stays
     1:1 (the close-out pair commits normally, then the
     merge rebase; chids survive rebase, so ochids stay
     valid); its enabler is the Todo "vc-x1 push: pause
     point between commit and publish stages".
   - Teach push to:
     - detect the shape (code WC empty, uncovered commits at
       the bookmark)
     - skip `commit-app`
     - compose a `.claude`-specific message
     - emit one `ochid:` line per uncovered commit
   - Open: computing "uncovered" — likely a revset from the
     code bookmark back to the newest commit referenced by
     the bot journal's ochids.
10. **Run validate-bot at every vc-x1 invocation
    (config-gated).** The check is one jj spawn
    (`jj bookmark list main --all-remotes`), cheap enough
    to run at every execution — noted 2026-07-15 as a
    "could, not should". Design points:
    - locate the bot repo (`<cwd>/.claude` or config;
      shares the lookup with the refactor program's
      [facade-owns-topology stage](refactor-20260716.md#stage-facade-owns-topology))
      and silently skip when absent
    - severity knob in `.vc-config.toml`
      (`warn|error|off`): unrelated commands (fix-todo)
      warn at most; push / squash-push / validate-bot
      already have their own handling from 0.69.0-3
11. **README: audit flag tables and examples against the
    current CLI.** 0.69.0-4 fixed the init section (it
    documented retired `--owner` / `--dir` / `--repo-local`
    flags) and the 0.69.0 surfaces, but the README's other
    tables (clone, symlink, sync, revert, list/desc/chid/
    show) have never had a systematic `-h` comparison and
    drift silently.
    - Sweep each section against `vc-x1 <cmd> -h`.
    - Consider regenerating transcripts via support
      scripts (the gen-exmpl pattern) so examples stay
      reproducible.

## Ideas

 Items not yet solid enough for `## Todo` (or surfaced
 during close-out / end-of-day before they're fully
 formed). Triaged at the next Preparation: promote to
 `## Todo` / `notes/todo-backlog.md`, fold into a
 picked-up cycle, or drop.

1. **Codify ochid invariant + bot-repo rules + squash
   gating + cross-repo migration in
   `notes/cycle-protocol.md`.** Was planned for
   `0.59.0-2` but deferred when 0.59.0 closed out via
   squash + Option F (manual bot-side rewrite). The
   rules were exercised manually for the close-out;
   this Idea formally codifies them.
   - Codify the **ochid invariant** in `## ochid
     trailers`: every public ochid must resolve in the
     public graph.
   - Codify bot-repo rules: never squashed; descriptions
     editable (`jj describe` preserves chid).
   - Codify squash gating: until `vc-x1 push --squash`
     exists, manual symmetric squash (Option F: app
     squash + bot-side trailer rewrite + force-push) is
     the standard recipe; merge non-ff is the default
     shape for multi-commit cycles (the concrete recipe is
     its own Todo — "merge-non-ff recipe").
   - Sketch cross-repo migration: ochids change at
     every merge until the change reaches the canonical
     repo's `main`.
   - Apply Ideas-aware Preparation/Close-out updates:
     Preparation triages `## Ideas` (promote / fold /
     drop) before declaring the plan; Close-out
     captures unresolved follow-ups into `## Ideas`.

2. **`vc` as a code+conversation provenance tool (grander
   ambition).** Today `vc-x1` manages a dual repo (code +
   `.claude`) cross-linked by `ochid:`. The larger aim is
   to *surface* that link — view history with the
   conversation and the code side by side: provenance, the
   *why* of a change, not just the *what*. The dual-repo +
   `ochid` design is already the substrate; the cross-links
   make code↔conversation navigable, so the viewer is UI
   over an already-solved data link.
   - Build direction: keep resolution/assembly in `vc` — an
     editor-agnostic Rust engine/lib extending the
     `show` / `chid` / `desc` family ("given a commit,
     resolve its ochid and assemble the paired diff +
     conversation slice"); the editor add-on is a thin
     presentation layer over it.
   - Front-end leans a Zed add-on (Rust, preferred), maybe
     VSCode / other. Verify Zed's extension API can host a
     rich side-by-side panel before committing — an
     editor-agnostic core hedges the bet.
   - `vc-x2`? A rewrite is unwarranted: the audit's
     Commonality pass found the architecture sound (por is
     bolted on where an existing good pattern wasn't
     applied) — equalize incrementally. "vc-x2" only makes
     sense if the viewer changes the *core* architecture
     (an index / daemon / data model). Separate
     engine-rewrite (no) from product-reposition (open).
   - Possible artifact: a top-level
     `notes/design-cli/vision.md` framing the direction,
     with the parity and conversion docs as sub-designs.
3. **Restructure the design-cli parity docs (target
   0.63.0).** `por-dual-parity-audit.md` (~1200 lines)
   fuses a *frozen* audit (the `## 1`–`## 8` snapshot
   evidence) with a *living* design (axes, decisions,
   matrix, gap list); the "audit" name undersells it and
   the halves have different lifecycles. And
   `por-dual-parity.md` (the stub) overlaps on parity but
   uniquely holds the `por ↔ dual` conversion design.
   - Split the audit doc into a frozen audit snapshot + a
     living design doc (names TBD; could reclaim
     `por-dual-parity.md` for the design).
   - Refocus the stub to conversion-only and rename (e.g.
     `por-dual-conversion.md`); drop its redundant parity
     half.
   - Repoint refs (`todo.md` `[1]` + the `por → dual` Todo,
     `copying.md`, the audit's internal anchors + Reading
     guide) and validate; `chores-10/11/12` mentions are
     historical and stay.
   - Promote the Gap-list items to anchored
     `#### Gap N — <title>` sub-headings so cross-cycle
     citations can deep-link a specific gap (markdown
     anchors headings, not list items). Trade-off: stable
     anchors, but the ordinal lives in the heading text
     (manual renumber on reorder) — fine for a consumed
     backlog. The 3 `Gap #N` links in the `0.62.0`
     close-out chores narrative resolve only to the section
     until this lands.
   - Deferred from the 0.62.0 close-out: close-out is
     bookkeeping-only, and the split is substantive,
     anchor-heavy work warranting its own cycle.

## Bugs

_See [bugs.md](bugs.md)._

## Done

Completed tasks are moved from `## Todo` to here, `## Done`, as they are completed
and older `## Done` sections are moved to [done.md](done.md) to keep this file small.

_Migrated to [done.md](done.md) on 2026-07-14 (0.51.0–0.65.2 batch)._

- feat: pin bot repo to main (0.68.0) — `--bookmark` is code-repo-only in push and sync; the session repo's side of every step (tracking preflight, classify/act, `bookmark-set` — renamed from `bookmark-both` — `finalize --push`, completion sanity) is pinned to `main`; plus two mid-cycle sync fixes: `reposition_session` no-ops when `@-` is the `main` tip, and the clean case prints one `nothing to sync` summary line [[23]]
- docs: diagnose silent session-push loss (0.68.1) — Bugs #1 root-caused: push's detached finalize child is killed at sandbox teardown before its delayed squash/push runs, so bot-run pushes never push `.claude`; diagnosis recorded in bugs.md, fix design queued as Todo #1 (inline session push + preflight backstop + finalize as the user's empty-@ tidy-up); 0.68.0 chores `Commits:` backfilled [[25]]
- feat: inline session push + squash-push (0.69.0) — push's session publish is in-process (`squash-push-bot` stage; a failure is a visible push failure — the silent session-push loss fixed); `finalize` renamed to the zero-ceremony `squash-push` (detach / delay / failure markers retired, no alias); new `vc-x1 validate-bot` + an erroring push-preflight backstop enforce the at-rest `main == main@origin` invariant (no auto-fix); push preflight drops the hardcoded cargo steps (vc-x1 assumes nothing about repo contents beyond `.jj` + `.vc-config.toml`); work/bot terminology + stage renames across code and docs, README rewritten (Terminology section, live-validated walkthroughs); crate renamed back to `vc-x1` [[20]]
- docs: shared protocol sync + jj refactor plan — adopted the vc-template-x1 shared notes set (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, versioning.md, jj-tips.md) with vc-x1's 0.69.0 corrections ratified template-side (manifest: [notes-sync-20260716.md](notes-sync-20260716.md)); jj facade → jj-lib refactor program planned in [refactor-20260716.md](refactor-20260716.md), absorbing eight Todos [[1]]

1	//! Shared core for the `validate-todo` / `fix-todo` subcommands:
2	//! parse `notes/todo.md`'s numbered sections and compute the
3	//! renumber / re-indent plan.
4	//!
5	//! - `## Todo` and `## Bugs` hold manually-numbered entries
6	//!   (`1.` `2.` … at column 0); every other section is skipped.
7	//! - `analyze` walks the file once: it returns one `Change` per
8	//!   entry whose number or continuation indent is off, plus the
9	//!   fully renumbered / re-indented file content.
10	//! - Continuation indent is normalized against each entry's
11	//!   *measured* base indent, so nested `- ` sub-bullets keep
12	//!   their relative depth.
13	
14	/// Default todo file, relative to the workspace root.
15	pub const TODO_FILE: &str = "notes/todo.md";
16	
17	/// A manually-numbered section of the todo file.
18	#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug)]
19	pub enum Section {
20	    /// The `## Todo` backlog list.
21	    Todo,
22	    /// The `## Bugs` list.
23	    Bugs,
24	}
25	
26	impl Section {
27	    /// The section's markdown header text (`## Todo` / `## Bugs`).
28	    pub fn header(self) -> &'static str {
29	        match self {
30	            Section::Todo => "## Todo",
31	            Section::Bugs => "## Bugs",
32	        }
33	    }
34	}
35	
36	/// One entry whose number and/or continuation indent needs to
37	/// change for the section to be sequentially numbered.
38	///
39	/// - `num_old` / `num_new` — the displayed number and its
40	///   sequential (1-based) position; equal when only the indent is

content.content:
1	# Notes
2	
3	This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
4	Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.
5	
6	By default there are todo.md and the chores-NN.md files in
7	[chores/](chores). Chores are general notes about tasks and
8	todo.md contains short term tasks and their status. The
9	chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; the highest-numbered

file.content:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are todo.md and the chores-NN.md files in
[chores/](chores). Chores are general notes about tasks and
todo.md contains short term tasks and their status. The
chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; the highest-numbered
file is the active one, older ones are closed.

Tool architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
*live status* is in the chores files) — lives at the repo root in
[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

Multi-cycle programs too big for a todo.md entry get their own
dated plan file — e.g.
[refactor-20260716.md](refactor-20260716.md) (typed jj facade →
jj-lib in-process): one `##` section per stage, so chores and
todo entries can reference a stage by anchor. The plan file owns
the forward design; chores still records each shipped cycle.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow and conventions live in
[`../AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md):

- [Notes file conventions](../AGENTS.md#notes-file-conventions)
  — Todo format, Reference numbering, Notes references
  (`[[N]]` citation style), Markdown anchor links, Retiring
  Done entries.
- [Chores conventions](../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions) —
  section headers / Done entries exact-title rule, content
  rules, `Commits:` line format.
- [Code Conventions](../AGENTS.md#code-conventions) — doc
  comments, `// OK: …` on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.

Per-cycle workflow lives in
[`cycle-protocol.md`](cycle-protocol.md):

- [Cycles](cycle-protocol.md#cycles) — three-phase shape
  (Preparation → Work → Close-out), `X.Y.Z-N` numbering,
  sub-cycles.
- [Per-commit flow](cycle-protocol.md#per-commit-flow) —
  cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`),
  work + commit description review gates.
- [Commit description](cycle-protocol.md#commit-description)
  — Conventional Commits + `(version)` suffix; body shape
  per work vs bot repo.
- [Pushing](cycle-protocol.md#pushing) — push policy,
  close-out shape, `.claude` cadence.

input.description: Show top of bugs.md

content.content:
# Bugs

Known defects we're aware of but haven't scheduled a fix for.
Each entry describes what goes wrong, when, and the cost of
the failure. Entries are numbered (`1.` `2.` …) the same way
as `## Todo` in `todo.md`; run
`vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run notes/bugs.md` to renumber after
insert / delete / reorder.

## Bugs

1. **`init --repo local` bare remotes keep HEAD at
   `refs/heads/master`.** The only branch pushed is `main`,
   so a later `jj git clone` of that bare repo has no default
   branch to auto-track and `vc-x1 clone` fails its
   `verify_tracking` check ("bookmark 'main' has non-tracking
   remote 'main@origin'"). Found building `tests/cli_sync.rs`
   (worked around there with `git symbolic-ref HEAD
   refs/heads/main`).
   - **Fix direction:** init's local bare provisioning sets
     HEAD to `refs/heads/main` at creation.

2. **`clone` session-remote derivation mismatches init's
   local naming; relative TARGET breaks the session clone.**
   Two related defects, both found building
   `tests/cli_sync.rs`:
   - `derive_session_url` maps `<x>.git` →
     `<x>.claude.git`, but `init --repo local` names the
     session remote `remote-claude.git` — so a dual clone of
     a locally-init'd project's `remote-code.git` looks for

file.content:
- 0.69.0-1 inline session push (`squash-push-bot` in-process)
- 0.69.0-2 `finalize` → `squash-push` rename; detach retired;
  protocol docs rewritten for the inline world
- 0.69.0-3 published backstop: `validate-bot`, erroring
  preflight, squash-push report; cargo preflight removed;
  crate renamed back to `vc-x1`
- 0.69.0-4 work/bot terminology + stage-name sweep; README
  rewrite; mismatch report suppressed mid-push; "finalize"
  scrubbed from `*.rs` and cycle-protocol.md
- 0.69.0 close-out: Bugs #1 pruned as fixed, index.lock race
  recorded, notes retired

## docs: shared protocol sync + jj refactor plan

Commits:

Two docs threads landed together (first title under the new
no-version-suffix convention — see versioning.md, adopted this
commit).

- Shared protocol sync: adopt the vc-template-x1 shared set —
  AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, versioning.md (new),
  jj-tips.md (new, supersedes jj-revsets.md) — with vc-x1's
  0.69.0 corrections applied and ratified template-side
  (inline squash-push flow restored, current stage names,
  preflight described as state-checks-only, roadmap material
  de-projected from the wrapper section). The per-file
  what/why is
  [notes-sync-20260716.md](../notes-sync-20260716.md); the
  goal is a byte-identical shared set across iiac-perf,
  vc-x1, vc-template-x1.
- jj refactor program: the `bookmark-set` index-lock race
  investigation (bugs.md entry 3) grew into a staged program
  — typed jj facade → jj-lib in-process, ending subprocess
  spawning — absorbing eight existing Todos; plan and design
  in [refactor-20260716.md](../refactor-20260716.md) (the
  first dated plan file, one `##` section per stage for
  stable anchors). todo.md restructured around it; the
  TODO.md-move entry added as Todo #1.

content.content:
docs: converge shared protocol doc set

vc-template-x1, vc-x1, and iiac-perf converge on one
byte-identical protocol doc set (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md,
jj-tips.md). vc-x1 was the canon for the protocol rewrite, so
this commit is exactly the four review fixes agreed across the
three sessions; notes/cycle-protocol.md already matched and is
unchanged.

- AGENTS.md: unwrap-convention section — the stray-site clippy
  sweep happens "in the per-commit flow", not "by preflight"
  (preflight runs no build steps, so the old line contradicted
  the push section).
- notes/jj-tips.md ("force" push example)
  - balance the backticks so `jj describe` and `vi README.md`
    render as two code spans
  - rewrite the garbled sentence introducing the complete
    example: "conventaional" typo and the "for of ... is
    should" run-on

Agreed sha256 (12-hex): AGENTS.md 5381e6c44181,
notes/cycle-protocol.md f76b5b39cbd1, notes/jj-tips.md
813d403db5ed.

input.new_string:
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md**

The todo list is the project's live state and the routine
acquaint read; root-level uppercase puts it in the
conventional root-file family (README, LICENSE, AGENTS.md,
ARCHITECTURE.md) — the same "easy for everyone to find"
argument that put AGENTS.md at the root. Siblings
(`todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`, `done.md`) stay in `notes/` —
TODO.md is the entry point, the README→docs/ shape.

- Decision (2026-07-16): shared AGENTS.md keeps hard paths
  (greppable) rather than naming the file abstractly.
- The AGENTS.md "File reads" section (and cycle-protocol.md's
  two mentions) are part of the shared byte-identical set, so
  this is a three-project change (vc-x1, vc-template-x1,
  iiac-perf) applied identically; vc-x1 goes first, breaking
  byte-identity until the other two apply the same change.
- Historical files (chores-NN.md, done.md, dated
  manifests/audits) keep their `notes/todo.md` mentions.

Plan:
- 0.69.2-0 docs: open TODO.md move cycle (current)
- 0.69.2-1 docs: move notes/todo.md to TODO.md
  - `mv` + jj rename detection; TODO.md's relative links
    gain `notes/` prefixes; inbound links update (AGENTS.md,
    cycle-protocol.md, README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md,
    notes/README.md, bugs.md, refactor-20260716.md, live
    design notes).
- 0.69.2-2 refactor: TODO.md as validate/fix-todo default
  - `TODO_FILE` const → `TODO.md` — the no-arg default must
    follow the move (a behavior change; the Todo entry's
    "code behavior is unaffected" held only for doc
    strings); src/ doc-string sweep + README default-path
    mentions.
- 0.69.2 docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md (close-out)

## Todo

   the project's live state and the routine acquaint read;
   root-level uppercase puts it in the conventional root-file
   family (README, LICENSE, AGENTS.md, ARCHITECTURE.md) —
   the same "easy for everyone to find" argument that put

     paths (greppable) rather than naming the file

72	   ~30 hand-rolled `run("jj", …)` spawns plus every
73	   mutation, with per-module private wrappers and raw-git
74	   vestiges in init — stderr parsing instead of typed
75	   errors, and jj's single-attempt index-lock acquisition
76	   (the push `bookmark-set` lock race in [bugs.md](bugs.md))
77	   can't be retried where it fails. A multi-ladder program;
78	   the staged plan, design detail, and the eight absorbed
79	   former Todos live in
80	   [refactor-20260716.md](refactor-20260716.md).
81	   - Stages in execution order: DRY facade → hygiene
82	     riders → facade owns topology → de-gitify init →
83	     split push.rs → jj-lib migration → push body-intro
84	     validation → trapezoid close-out → por → dual
85	     conversion.
86	2. **vc-x1 push: pause point between commit and publish
87	   stages.** The merge non-ff close-out is a three-step
88	   sequence:
89	   - commit the close-out pair locally (normal 1:1 commit
90	     stages, ochids injected both directions)
91	   - rebase the code-side close-out into the merge (chids
92	     survive rebase, so every ochid stays valid)
93	   - publish
94	
95	   Push has no supported stop after `commit-bot`, so today
96	   the recipe pre-commits both sides manually and resumes via
97	   `--from bookmark-set --yes` — skipping exactly the stages
98	   that inject `ochid:` trailers.
99	   - Add a stop after the commit stages (`--to commit-bot`
100	     or `--no-publish`; name open); the existing `--from
101	     bookmark-set` is already the resume half.
102	   - Retires the close-out workaround; the merge commit
103	     carries its code→bot ochid because it was injected
104	     normally, before the rebase.
105	   - Together with the Todo "push/sync: bookmark is
106	     code-repo-only; pin the bot repo to main", completes
107	     the trapezoidal-commit workflow (1:1 bot↔code
108	     throughout; the merge is a code-side-only shape
109	     operation).
110	   - Interim recipe only: the refactor program's
111	     [trapezoid close-out stage](refactor-20260716.md#stage-trapezoid-close-out)
112	     is the end state; this pause point remains the manual
113	     path until it lands.
114	
115	3. **Version-number protocol is fragile — versions are
116	   baked into titles/bodies/todo/done/chores before the
117	   change lands.** The cycle protocol embeds an `X.Y.Z-N`
118	   version in commit titles and bodies, `## Todo` /
119	   `## Done` entries, and chores headers — all written
120	   while the work is in progress, i.e. before it lands.
121	   But version numbers are subject to change: in a public,
122	   merge-based flow (e.g. Linux), the version a change
123	   ships under is only fixed when it merges into `main`,
124	   so the landing version can't be anticipated while the
125	   work is underway. Pervasive version-in-text is
126	   therefore fragile for any non-linear / multi-contributor
127	   workflow. Promoted from Ideas at 0.65.2-0; slated for
128	   the cycle after 0.65.2.
129	   - Open question: what identifies a cycle's commits if
130	     not a pre-assigned version?
131	     - Needs to be unique within some agreed upon domain.
132	       A contributors email address would do it, but also
133	       a UUID (short-version) for a contribution. I could
134	       imagine a UUID generated from the initial email/issue
135	       that and then "version number" schema appended to that.
136	   - Surfaces to update once the identifier is chosen:
137	     cycle-protocol.md (title shape, Numbering), AGENTS.md
138	     (commit-recording headers), and the `vc-x1` validators
139	     that parse `(X.Y.Z)` strings.
140	4. **sync follow-up: extract `move-bookmark` command.** The
141	   "put the bookmark / `@` where it belongs" step at the end
142	   of sync (reposition logic) is useful standalone — e.g. the
143	   t1B scenario where `main` is right but `@` isn't on it —
144	   and deserves an honestly-named command instead of a mode.
145	   - `vc-x1 move-bookmark` (name open): no fetch; move `@`
146	     (and optionally the bookmark) onto a target under the
147	     same safety rules as sync's reposition step.
148	   - Sync's final step becomes a call to the same logic.
149	   - Follow-up to the 0.67.0 single-mode sync cycle.
150	5. **sync follow-up: push preflight in-process; drop
151	   `--check`; revisit push auto-rollback.** Push's preflight
152	   shells out to `vc-x1 sync --check` — a verify-only pass
153	   that is both racy (remote can move before the user's
154	   later apply) and not actually read-only (jj's fetch
155	   auto-ffs tracked bookmarks). Follow-up to the 0.67.0
156	   single-mode sync cycle.
157	   - Preflight becomes a real in-process sync (stop-on-error
158	     halts the push before anything is committed); the
159	     `sync --check` shell-out and PATH dependency go away.
160	   - Remove the deprecated hidden `--check` alias once
161	     nothing invokes it.
162	   - Apply the stop-on-error + `vc-x1 revert` philosophy to

... [225 lines truncated] ...

Preparation (0.69.2-0) work is complete — ready for your review. `fmt` and `clippy` pass; the working-copy diff is:

- **Cargo.toml / Cargo.lock** — version → `0.69.2-0`.
- **notes/todo.md** — Todo #1 picked up into `## In Progress` (problem statement + ladder below), remaining entries renumbered 1–10 (`fix-todo`), both files validate.
- **notes/chores/chores-13.md** — new section `## docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md` opened with empty `Commits:`; the pending sync section backfilled.

The planned ladder:

- `0.69.2-1 docs: move notes/todo.md to TODO.md` — the move plus the markdown reference sweep (live docs only; historical chores/done/audits keep `notes/todo.md`).
- `0.69.2-2 refactor: TODO.md as validate/fix-todo default` — `TODO_FILE` const + src doc strings + README default-path mentions.
- `0.69.2` close-out.

Three judgment calls to confirm:

1. **`TODO_FILE` default changes to `TODO.md`** (step -2). The Todo entry claimed "code behavior is unaffected", but that's only true for doc strings — `TODO_FILE` is the clap `default_value` for `validate-todo`/`fix-todo`, so the no-arg default must follow the move or it breaks in all three projects. I've noted this correction in the ladder.
2. **Chores backfill of the converge commit.** `66bc1f2` ("docs: converge shared protocol doc set") landed with no chores section of its own. Since it's the review round of the shared-sync thread, I recorded it on that section's `Commits: [[31]],[[32]]` with a one-line note, rather than opening a retroactive section.
3. **Byte-identity break.** This cycle edits AGENTS.md and cycle-protocol.md, which the converge commit just made byte-identical (with agreed sha256s) across vc-x1 / vc-template-x1 / iiac-perf. vc-x1 goes first per the Todo's decision note; the other two projects need the identical change afterward — that follow-up isn't part of this cycle.

Ready to commit.

input.description: Show vc-x1 push flags

content.content:
vc-x1 0.69.0

Commit both repos, push the work repo's BOOKMARK, and
squash-push the bot repo's `main` — one resumable command.

Collapses the manual commit-push-publish ceremony into a
single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a
state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can
resume without re-doing completed stages.

Stages: preflight (tracking / bot-published / sync checks —
no build steps; run project checks yourself before pushing)
→ review (approve diff)
→ message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →
commit-work → commit-bot (skipped if clean) → bookmark-set
(work repo → <bookmark>, bot repo → main) → push-work →
squash-push-bot. Failures in commit-work / commit-bot /
bookmark-set roll both repos back via
`jj op restore` to the snapshot recorded before commit-work.
After push-work succeeds the remote boundary is crossed and
recovery is forward-only.

Non-interactive use: pass both --title and --body plus --yes
to skip the review gate. Saved state carries title/body
across resumes so only the first invocation needs them.

Usage: vc-x1 push [OPTIONS] [BOOKMARK]

Arguments:
  [BOOKMARK]
          Bookmark to advance in the work repo (positional form of
          `--bookmark`). The bot repo always advances `main`.
          
          Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
          without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an alias
          for scripts and for composition with other args. The two forms
          conflict if both supplied.

Options:
      --bookmark <BOOKMARK>
          Bookmark to advance in the work repo (flag form; see positional)

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
          
          Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after printing): the
          banner now rides along with normal subcommand execution rather than
          gating it, so scripts can capture the version *and* the command's
          output in one invocation.

      --restart
          Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --from <STAGE>
          Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use)

          Possible values:
          - preflight:       Verify bookmark tracking, the bot-published
            invariant, and sync state
          - review:          Present diff for the first approval gate
          - message:         Compose / edit the commit message; present for
            second gate
          - commit-work:     Commit the work repo
          - commit-bot:      Commit the `.claude` bot repo (skipped if empty)
          - bookmark-set:    Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: work →
            `<bookmark>`, bot → `main`
          - push-work:       `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`
          - squash-push-bot: In-process squash of `.claude`'s trailing session
            writes + push `main`

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --step
          Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate

      --status
          Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit

      --recheck
          Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run
          succeeded)

      --no-squash-push
          Stop before `squash-push-bot` so it can be run manually

      --dry-run
          Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects

      --title <STR>
          Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

      --body <STR>
          Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

  -y, --yes
          Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
          confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both supplied
          in non-interactive contexts

  -h, --help
          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

          Print help (see a summary with '-h')

78	
79	## File reads — read the slice you need
80	
81	Long notes files are appended to over time. Read only the
82	slice your task needs; grep or read further on demand.
83	
84	- **`notes/todo.md`** (the routine acquaint read) — first
85	  ~60 lines covers intro + `## In Progress` + the top of the
86	  ranked `## Todo` (priorities, #1 highest). `Read` with
87	  `offset=0, limit=60`. `## Ideas` sits below `## Todo`; read
88	  further only when chasing a lower-ranked entry, an Idea, a
89	  `[N]` ref, or auditing the whole list.
90	- **`notes/todo-backlog.md`** — the long-tail backlog
91	  (lower-priority entries below the ranked `## Todo`). Read
92	  only when picking up a backlog item; grep to locate it
93	  first.
94	- **`notes/bugs.md`** — the bug list. Small; read whole
95	  when triaging a bug or chasing the `## Bugs` pointer in
96	  todo.md.
97	- **`notes/done.md`** + **`notes/chores/chores-NN.md`** —
98	  historical / append-mostly. Scan headings first
99	  (`grep '^## ' notes/chores/chores-NN.md`), then read only
100	  the section you need.
101	
102	**Why:** the routine read should stay small. `notes/todo.md`
103	grows every cycle, so the backlog and bugs live in sibling
104	files rather than inline; the same "slice you need" rule
105	applies to historical files.
106	
107	## Memory
108	
109	Do not use the bot's per-project memory directory
110	(`~/.claude/projects/<path>/memory/`). In a dual-repo setup with
111	AGENTS.md it provides no capability AGENTS.md doesn't already cover,
112	and it loses on discoverability:

file.content:
## File reads — read the slice you need

Long notes files are appended to over time. Read only the
slice your task needs; grep or read further on demand.

- **`notes/todo.md`** (the routine acquaint read) — first
  ~60 lines covers intro + `## In Progress` + the top of the
  ranked `## Todo` (priorities, #1 highest). `Read` with
  `offset=0, limit=60`. `## Ideas` sits below `## Todo`; read
  further only when chasing a lower-ranked entry, an Idea, a
  `[N]` ref, or auditing the whole list.
- **`notes/todo-backlog.md`** — the long-tail backlog
  (lower-priority entries below the ranked `## Todo`). Read
  only when picking up a backlog item; grep to locate it
  first.
- **`notes/bugs.md`** — the bug list. Small; read whole
  when triaging a bug or chasing the `## Bugs` pointer in
  todo.md.
- **`notes/done.md`** + **`notes/chores/chores-NN.md`** —
  historical / append-mostly. Scan headings first
  (`grep '^## ' notes/chores/chores-NN.md`), then read only
  the section you need.

**Why:** the routine read should stay small. `notes/todo.md`
grows every cycle, so the backlog and bugs live in sibling
files rather than inline; the same "slice you need" rule
applies to historical files.

## Memory

Do not use the bot's per-project memory directory
(`~/.claude/projects/<path>/memory/`). In a dual-repo setup with
AGENTS.md it provides no capability AGENTS.md doesn't already cover,
and it loses on discoverability:

attachment.snippet:
1	# Architecture
2	
3	How the `vc-x1` binary is structured: the clap-aware CLI layer
4	and the (emerging) clap-free subcommand layer. This is the
5	"what + some why" — generic and slow-moving. The *how* and the
6	per-cycle / per-subcommand status live in `notes/chores/chores-*.md`
7	and `TODO.md`.
8	
9	This is a living document; the tool is under continuous
10	development and the repo will keep changing.
11	
12	## Overview
13	
14	`vc-x1` is a single binary that supports subcommands,
15	`vc-x1 <subcommand>`, for managing a jj-git **dual-repo workspace**
16	with a work repo plus a `.claude` bot repo. The commits
17	cross-reference each other via `ochid:` trailers at the bottom
18	of each commit message. See [`README.md`](README.md) for the
19	user-facing picture and [`AGENTS.md`](AGENTS.md) for the bot
20	workflow.
21	
22	## Two layers: CLI args vs subcommand Context + Params
23	
24	Goal: separate clap-aware argument parsing from subcommand
25	operation logic, so a future front-end (TUI, library embedding)
26	can call the same core without dragging clap along. The trigger
27	was `args.account.account`-style nested-leaf access leaking into
28	subcommand bodies; accessor shortcuts were rejected as hiding
29	the mismatch rather than fixing it.
30	
31	- **CLI layer (clap-aware)**: builds `XxxParams` from the
32	  user-facing `XxxArgs` `#[derive(Args)]` clap struct.
33	  Options/flags used in more than one subcommand live as
34	  flattened leaves/bundles in `src/options_flags/`.
35	- **Subcommand layer (clap-free)**: entrypoint shape
36	  `fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams) -> Result<…>` —
37	  isolates the subcommand from clap and from the user.
38	  - `Context` — platform state shared by every subcommand.
39	  - `XxxParams` — that subcommand's inputs, derived from
40	    config / command-line args.
41	- **Boundary conversion**: `XxxArgs → XxxParams` via
42	  `impl From<&XxxArgs>` or `impl TryFrom<&XxxArgs>`, depending
43	  on whether the conversion can fail.
44	
45	Two parameters per subcommand; the signature documents what the
46	subcommand depends on. `Context` is the same across all
47	subcommands; `XxxParams` carry the per-subcommand inputs.
48	
49	Rules for the subcommand layer (some still aspirational — see
50	[the Context+Params port](#args--context--params)):
51	
52	1. Plain options, flat fields, `Default` — no clap types in
53	   `XxxParams`. Domain types (`RepoSelector`, `Scope`) are
54	   fine; leaf wrappers (`RepoOption`) are not.
55	2. Typed errors (`enum XError`, not `Box<dyn Error>`) — a TUI
56	   matches variants to pick dialogs; CLI formats them.
57	3. Returned outcomes, not `println!` — each subcommand returns
58	   a structured result; CLI formats it, a library writes
59	   nothing to stdout itself.
60	4. Progress via an optional `&mut dyn ProgressSink` on
61	   long-running subcommands — CLI installs a stderr sink, tests
62	   a recording sink.
63	5. No globals, no implicit cwd/env reads in subcommands — CLI
64	   resolves cwd/env once at startup and builds `Context`.
65	
66	None of this is sacred — the whole structure is an experiment.
67	The current scope is deliberately narrow: a small `Context`
68	(two params, not one god blob); no trait-based DI yet; one
69	crate, not a workspace split; `src/options_flags/` leaves left
70	in place — only their consumers change shape. Widen any of it
71	when the evidence (a second front-end, a second consumer crate,
72	a real platform-surface divergence) calls for it — not before.
73	
74	### Naming
75	
76	Earlier drafts of this design used `Workspace` / `XOptions`;
77	the implementation (0.44.0) chose `Context` / `XxxParams`:
78	
79	- `Workspace` → `Context` — Cargo owns "workspace" formally,
80	  and this codebase already uses "workspace" for the dual-repo
81	  project root (`find_workspace_root`).
82	- `XOptions` → `XxxParams` — avoids visual collision with
83	  `Option<T>` (a params struct mixes required and
84	  `Option<T>`-typed fields) and with `src/options_flags/`.
85	
86	## Module map
87	
88	The *kinds* of module and what they're for — not an exhaustive
89	catalog (read the `//!` docstrings; `notes/chores/chores-*.md` for
90	per-subcommand refactor status):
91	
92	**CLI layer:**
93	
94	- `src/main.rs` — the clap edge: `Cli` (`Parser`), `Commands`
95	  (`Subcommand`) + the dispatch arms, `cli_with_banner()` (the
96	  per-subcommand `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner), `CompleteEnv` wiring,
97	  and `bm_track` (the permanent `main`-bookmark tracking
98	  diagnostic on every command's entry/exit).
99	- `src/options_flags/` — reusable `#[derive(Args)]` *leaves*
100	  (one flag, or a small fixed pair) and *bundles*
101	  (`#[command(flatten)]` of N leaves) shared across
102	  subcommands, so a flag is defined (parser, help, completer)
103	  exactly once. See
104	  [`src/options_flags/README.md`](src/options_flags/README.md).
105	- `src/common.rs` — helpers the read-only commit-query
106	  subcommands (`chid` / `desc` / `list` / `show`) share:
107	  `for_each_repo`, `collect_ids`, `resolve_revset`, the
108	  `DotSpec` / `..`-notation parsing, `Header` /
109	  `resolve_header`, the `format_*` printers, and the
110	  `-R`/`-s` resolution stack — `find_workspace_root` /
111	  `default_scope` / `scope_to_repos` / `resolve_repos`
112	  (the top-level entry combining the `-R PATH` override and
113	  the `-s code|bot|code,bot` role selection).
114	
115	**Subcommand-layer scaffolding:**
116	
117	- `src/context.rs` — `Context`, the shared platform handle
118	  (today: the loaded `UserConfig` + the `--log` path; built
119	  once at startup via `Context::load(log)`).
120	- `src/config.rs` — `UserConfig` (`~/.config/vc-x1/config.toml`).
121	- `src/options_flags/scope.rs` — `Scope` (a newtype over
122	  `Vec<Side>` — the `code`/`bot` role set) and `parse_scope`.
123	
124	**Subcommand modules** — `src/<x>.rs` holds the subcommand's
125	`XxxArgs` (`#[derive(Args)]`, composing `options_flags/` leaves
126	where one exists) and the entrypoint `pub fn x(...)`. Modules:
127	`chid`, `desc` (+ `desc_helpers`), `list`, `show`,
128	`validate_desc`, `fix_desc`, `validate_todo` / `fix_todo`
129	(+ `todo_helpers`), `clone`, `init` (+ `init/params`),
130	`symlink`, `sync`, `finalize`, `push` (a resumable state
131	machine). Which ones have ported to `(ctx, params)` and which
132	compose `options_flags/` leaves: `notes/chores/chores-*.md` +
133	`TODO.md`.
134	
135	**Support:**
136	
137	- `src/repo_utils.rs`, `src/url.rs`, `src/toml_simple.rs`,
138	  `src/logging.rs` — jj/repo helpers, URL parsing, a minimal
139	  TOML reader, the CLI logger.
140	- `src/test_helpers.rs`, `src/test_tmp_root.rs` —
141	  `#[cfg(test)]` fixtures and tempdir resolution. Subcommand
142	  modules also carry their own `#[cfg(test)] mod tests` (and
143	  `integration_tests` for `sync` / `push`).
144	
145	## Subcommand model
146	
147	Adding a subcommand `x`:
148	
149	1. Create `src/x.rs` with `pub struct XxxArgs`
150	   (`#[derive(Args)]`), composing leaves from
151	   `src/options_flags/` where one exists.
152	2. Add a variant `X(x::XxxArgs)` to `Commands` in `src/main.rs`
153	   with a `///` summary (and `#[command(long_about = …)]` for
154	   the long form).
155	3. Add a dispatch arm in `main()` calling `x::x(&args)` (or,
156	   once ported, `x::x(&ctx, &params)`).
157	4. Doc comments on `#[arg(...)]` fields drive `--help`; add
158	   `#[arg(verbatim_doc_comment, …)]` on any field whose doc
159	   comment uses bullets (clap otherwise reflows them into
160	   prose).
161	5. Dynamic value completion (`--account=<TAB>`) is an
162	   `ArgValueCompleter` attached to the leaf in
163	   `src/options_flags/` — completion is a clap-aware concern
164	   and stays at this layer.
165	
166	## args → Context + Params
167	
168	"The Context+Params port" — each subcommand's
169	`pub fn x(args: &XxxArgs)` is structured as
170	`pub fn x(ctx: &Context, params: &XxxParams)`, with an
171	`XxxParams` flat struct + a `From` (or `TryFrom`, if the
172	conversion is fallible) at the binary edge. `init` (0.44.0)
173	is the worked example; complete across all subcommands as of
174	0.49.0, and the shape new subcommands follow. Out of scope

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Notes

This directory contains various notes and documentation related to the project.
Each file is organized by topic for easy reference.

By default there are todo.md and the chores-NN.md files in
[chores/](chores). Chores are general notes about tasks and
todo.md contains short term tasks and their status. The
chores-NN files are numbered in sequence; the highest-numbered
file is the active one, older ones are closed.

Tool architecture — the CLI-args / `Context`+`Params` split, a
generic module map, the subcommand model, and what the
Context+Params port and the options_flags extraction *are* (the
*live status* is in the chores files) — lives at the repo root in
[`../ARCHITECTURE.md`](../ARCHITECTURE.md).

Multi-cycle programs too big for a todo.md entry get their own
dated plan file — e.g.
[refactor-20260716.md](refactor-20260716.md) (typed jj facade →
jj-lib in-process): one `##` section per stage, so chores and
todo entries can reference a stage by anchor. The plan file owns
the forward design; chores still records each shipped cycle.

In the future we I expect we may want to create a "notes"
database to better manage the information, TBD.

Examples chore file:
```
# Chores-01.md
 
General maintenance tasks and considerations for the project see other files for
more specific topics. A chore in a chores file provides quick information on the
how and why of a particular chore.

## Create a binary that lists jj info 

This binary should list the changeID, commitID, and description title
and using `jj-lib`
```

## Workflow and conventions

Bot-facing workflow and conventions live in
[`../AGENTS.md`](../AGENTS.md):

- [Notes file conventions](../AGENTS.md#notes-file-conventions)
  — Todo format, Reference numbering, Notes references
  (`[[N]]` citation style), Markdown anchor links, Retiring
  Done entries.
- [Chores conventions](../AGENTS.md#chores-conventions) —
  section headers / Done entries exact-title rule, content
  rules, `Commits:` line format.
- [Code Conventions](../AGENTS.md#code-conventions) — doc
  comments, `// OK: …` on `unwrap*` calls, ask-on-ambiguity,
  stuck detection.

Per-cycle workflow lives in
[`cycle-protocol.md`](cycle-protocol.md):

- [Cycles](cycle-protocol.md#cycles) — three-phase shape
  (Preparation → Work → Close-out), `X.Y.Z-N` numbering,
  sub-cycles.
- [Per-commit flow](cycle-protocol.md#per-commit-flow) —
  cargo cycle (`fmt` / `clippy` / `test` / `install`),
  work + commit description review gates.
- [Commit description](cycle-protocol.md#commit-description)
  — Conventional Commits + `(version)` suffix; body shape
  per work vs bot repo.
- [Pushing](cycle-protocol.md#pushing) — push policy,
  close-out shape, `.claude` cadence.

content.text:
0.69.2-1 work is complete — ready for your review. The working-copy diff:

- **`notes/todo.md` → `TODO.md`** — jj detected the rename (`R {notes/todo.md => TODO.md}`); its 9 relative links gained `notes/` prefixes, the intro's `fix-todo` command and two self-mentions now say `TODO.md`, and `-1` is marked done in the ladder.
- **AGENTS.md** — all ~15 mentions swept to `TODO.md`; the "Why" line reworded since backlog/bugs are no longer *sibling* files ("live in files under `notes/`").
- **notes/cycle-protocol.md** — both mentions (per-commit flow step 1, push-wrapper section).
- **README.md** — the future-enhancement mention and the task-tracking paragraph (reworded — near-term tasks no longer "live under notes/").
- **ARCHITECTURE.md** — three mentions including the reference-list link.
- **notes/README.md** — intro reworked (todo list now described as living at the repo root), plan-file paragraph updated.
- **notes/bugs.md, todo-backlog.md, done.md (intro line), draft-reviews.md, bot-data-formats.md, design-cli/por-dual-parity.md** — live pointers updated.
- **Cargo.toml/lock** — `0.69.2-1`.

Intentionally untouched: historical files (chores-NN, done.md body, notes-sync/init-clone/audit records, README's example transcript at line 219), README's `path/to/todo.md` generic examples, and everything under `src/` plus README line 316 ("the default") — those are `-2`'s scope. A full-repo grep confirms no stragglers outside those categories. Both numbered files validate; `validate-todo`'s no-arg default is knowingly broken until `-2` lands.

Ready to commit.

attachment.snippet:
1	# vc-x1
2	
3	- [Overview](#vc-x1)
4	- [Architecture](ARCHITECTURE.md)
5	- [Terminology](#terminology)
6	- [Usage](#usage)
7	  - [Revision shortcuts](#revision-shortcuts)
8	  - [Shell completion](#shell-completion)
9	  - [validate-desc](#validate-desc)
10	  - [fix-desc](#fix-desc)
11	  - [validate-todo](#validate-todo)
12	  - [fix-todo](#fix-todo)
13	  - [validate-bot](#validate-bot)
14	  - [clone](#clone)
15	  - [init](#init)
16	  - [symlink](#symlink)
17	  - [sync](#sync)
18	  - [revert](#revert)
19	  - [squash-push](#squash-push)
20	  - [push](#push)
21	  - [Testing push + squash-push](#testing-push--squash-push)
22	  - [Testing the ochid-trailer guard](#testing-the-ochid-trailer-guard)
23	- [Cross-repo Linking with Git Trailers](#cross-repo-linking-with-git-trailers)
24	- [jj Tips for Git Users](#jj-tips-for-git-users)
25	- [Thoughts for the future](#thoughts-for-the-future)
26	- [Contributing](#contributing)
27	- [License](#license)
28	
29	This is experiment 1 to explore creating a Vibe Coding (vc) environment.
30	We will investigate ways of using the dual jj-git repo concept, explored
31	in [hw-jjg-bot](https://github.com/winksaville/hw-jjg-bot.git) to
32	initially make it easy to see how the code base evolved. This is
33	made possible by the fact that we have two repos one with the code
34	and one with the conversation with the bot.
35	
36	I've chosen the jj-git environment because jj provides the concept that
37	each commit has an immutable changeID as well as the mutable commitID
38	of git. The idea is that each commit made on repo A writes the
39	changeID in the commit message to repo B. Thus there is a cross reference
40	between the two repos and this will allow vc-x1 to show how the repo
41	evolved and the entity (bot or human) can more clearly understand **how** and
42	most importantly **why** the code evolved.
43	
44	The solution space is wide open, from trivial CLI, web or app based
45	(mobile/non-mobile). In addition, I could see this as an extension to
46	existing programming editors like vscode and zed or even creating our
47	own IDE for vc.
48	
49	See [Initial commit with dual jj-git repos](./notes/chores/chores-01.md#initial-commit-with-dual-jj-git-repos)
50	for how the initial commit was created with the dual jj-git repos. After
51	doing so and I then created this README.md file.
52	
53	## Terminology
54	
55	A vc-x1 workspace pairs two jj-git repos, and this README names
56	their two sides **work** and **bot**:
57	
58	- **work repo** — the repo at the project root, holding the work
59	  product itself (source code, docs — the repo you would have
60	  anyway).
61	- **bot repo** — the `.claude` repo nested inside it, holding the
62	  bot's session data (the conversation that produced the work).
63	- **work** / **bot** — the two sides as roles, used wherever a
64	  command or doc needs to say which repo it means (e.g. push's
65	  `commit-work` / `commit-bot` stages).
66	
67	One historical wrinkle: the `-s`/`--scope` flag still spells the
68	work side `code` (`-s code`, `-s code,bot`) — that keyword predates
69	the work/bot naming.
70	
71	## Usage
72	
73	```
74	vc-x1 list [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # List commits in a jj repo
75	vc-x1 desc [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show full description of a commit
76	vc-x1 chid [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Print changeID(s) for a revision
77	vc-x1 show [-r REVISION] [-n COMMITS]  # Show commit details and diff summary
78	vc-x1 validate-desc [OPTS]                 # Validate commit descriptions
79	vc-x1 fix-desc [OPTS]                     # Fix commit descriptions (dry-run default)
80	vc-x1 validate-todo [FILE]                # Check todo-file entry numbering
81	vc-x1 fix-todo [FILE]                     # Renumber todo file (dry-run default)
82	vc-x1 validate-bot [OPTS]                 # Check the bot repo is published
83	vc-x1 clone <REPO> [NAME] [OPTS]          # Clone a dual-repo project
84	vc-x1 init <TARGET> [OPTS]                # Create a new dual-repo project
85	vc-x1 symlink [TARGET] [OPTS]             # Create Claude Code project symlink
86	vc-x1 sync [OPTS]                          # Fetch + sync both repos to their remotes
87	vc-x1 revert [OPTS]                        # Restore repos to a prior operation
88	vc-x1 squash-push [BOOKMARK] [OPTS]        # Squash @ into @-, advance a bookmark, push
89	vc-x1 push [BOOKMARK] [OPTS]               # Commit both repos, push work, squash-push bot
90	vc-x1 --version                            # Print version
91	vc-x1 --help                           # Print help
92	```
93	
94	**The `..` notation:** dots on the revision show which direction to
95	list commits:
96	
97	- `x..` — x at top, ancestors below (older commits)
98	- `..x` — descendants above (newer commits), x at bottom
99	- `..x..` — both directions, x in the middle
100	
101	COMMITS is the total number of commits to show (x is always included).
102	Without dots, `x` with a count defaults to `x..` (ancestors).
103	For `..x..`, the budget is split: ancestors get the extra on odd counts.
104	
105	```
106	vc-x1 list -r @              # just @ (1 commit)
107	vc-x1 list -r @.. -n 5       # @ + 4 ancestors (5 commits, @ at top)
108	vc-x1 list -r ..@ -n 3       # 2 descendants + @ (3 commits, @ at bottom)
109	vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 3     # 1 descendant, @, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
110	vc-x1 list -r ..@.. -n 4     # 1 descendant, @, 2 ancestors (4 commits)
111	```
112	
113	### Shell completion
114	
115	vc-x1 provides tab completion for all subcommands and flags using claps
116	[unstable-dynamic](https://docs.rs/clap_complete/latest/clap_complete/env/struct.CompleteEnv.html)
117	only feature. It is a "simple" implementation and does not handle completing
118	revisions but still useful. To enable it, add one of the following to your shell's startup file:
119	
120	```bash
121	# bash (~/.bashrc)
122	source <(COMPLETE=bash vc-x1)
123	
124	# zsh (~/.zshrc)
125	source <(COMPLETE=zsh vc-x1)
126	
127	# fish (~/.config/fish/config.fish)
128	source (COMPLETE=fish vc-x1 | psub)
129	```
130	
131	Completions are generated dynamically by the binary, so they stay in
132	sync with the installed version automatically.
133	
134	### Positional shorthand
135	
136	REVISION and COMMITS can be given as positional arguments, omitting
137	`-r` and `-n`:
138	
139	```
140	vc-x1 list x                # just x (1 commit)
141	vc-x1 list x 5              # x.. 5 → x + 4 ancestors (5 commits)
142	vc-x1 list x.. 5            # same as above
143	vc-x1 list ..x 3            # 2 descendants + x (3 commits)
144	vc-x1 list ..x.. 3          # 1 descendant, x, 1 ancestor (3 commits)
145	vc-x1 list ..x.. 1          # just x (1 commit)
146	```
147	
148	**Defaults:** with no arguments, REVISION is `@` and COMMITS is 1
149	(just the working copy).
150	
151	Named flags `-r`/`--revision`, `-n`/`--commits`, `-R`/`--repo`, and
152	`-s`/`--scope` take precedence over positional arguments.
153	
154	### Multi-repo queries
155	
156	`-s`/`--scope` selects workspace sides by role keyword; `code` is the
157	work repo, `bot` is the `.claude` repo, `code,bot` is both. The
158	workspace root is found by walking up from cwd (the existing
159	`find_workspace_root` rule).
160	
161	```
162	vc-x1 chid -s code,bot          # both sides of the workspace
163	vc-x1 chid -s code              # just the work repo
164	vc-x1 chid -s bot               # just the bot repo
165	vc-x1 list @.. 3 -s code,bot    # works with chid / desc / list / show
166	```
167	
168	`-R`/`--repo` takes a single path. Used alone it points at one jj
169	repo (workspace lookup is skipped); combined with `-s` it overrides
170	the workspace root for the role lookup:
171	
172	```
173	vc-x1 chid -R .                  # single repo at .
174	vc-x1 chid -R .claude            # single repo at .claude
175	vc-x1 chid -R ../other -s code,bot   # both sides of ../other workspace
176	```
177	
178	Defaults preserve prior behavior — no flag → `[.]`, `-R foo` alone
179	→ `[foo]`. When multiple repos are resolved (any `-s` that names
180	more than one role), output is labeled with bold `=== path ===`
181	headers by default. Control the label with `-l`/`--label` and
182	`-L`/`--no-label`:
183
