input.description: List todo_helpers test names and wrapper sizes

content.text:
The core logic is tested, but the subcommand wrappers are not:

- **`todo_helpers.rs` — 15 tests** covering the analyze/renumber engine: gap renumbering, continuation-indent shifts with prefix width, nested sub-bullet depth preservation, Bugs numbered independently of Todo, `## Done` not scanned, idempotence of a fixed file, etc.
- **`desc_helpers.rs` — 22 tests** covering the description-validation core the same way.
- **`validate_todo.rs` / `fix_todo.rs` / `validate_desc.rs` / `fix_desc.rs` — 0 tests each.** They're thin wrappers (112–407 lines): clap arg structs, read the file, call the helper's analyze, print/write. Nothing exercises them at the CLI level either — no integration test invokes these subcommands.

So what's uncovered is exactly the wrapper layer: file I/O, output formatting, exit codes — and notably the no-arg default path (`default_value = TODO_FILE`), which is the thing this cycle just changed. I verified it manually end-to-end, but no test would catch a regression there.

A natural place to close that gap is the existing Todo #6 ("validate-numbering: rename the pair, check all sequence-managed notes files generically") — it already plans to restructure this pair, so wrapper/CLI tests could ride along, or be added as a bullet there. Want me to note that in the Todo entry, or leave it for when #6 is picked up?

input.old_string:
## docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md

input.new_string:
## docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md

Commits: [[33]],[[34]],[[35]]

The todo list is the project's live state and the routine
acquaint read; root-level uppercase puts it in the
conventional root-file family (README, LICENSE, AGENTS.md,
ARCHITECTURE.md) — the same "easy for everyone to find"
argument that put AGENTS.md at the root. Siblings
(`todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`, `done.md`) stay in `notes/` —
TODO.md is the entry point, the README→docs/ shape.

- Decision (2026-07-16): shared AGENTS.md keeps hard paths
  (greppable) rather than naming the file abstractly.
- The AGENTS.md "File reads" section (and cycle-protocol.md's
  two mentions) are part of the shared byte-identical set, so
  this is a three-project change (vc-x1, vc-template-x1,
  iiac-perf) applied identically; vc-x1 went first, breaking
  byte-identity until the other two apply the same change.
- Historical files (chores-NN.md, done.md, dated
  manifests/audits) keep their `notes/todo.md` mentions.
- The no-arg `validate-todo` / `fix-todo` default
  (`TODO_FILE`) had to follow the move — a behavior change
  the original Todo entry's "code behavior is unaffected"
  parenthetical missed (it held only for doc strings).
- Surfaced in review: the validate/fix wrapper layer has no
  tests (the analyze cores do) — noted as a bullet on the
  validate-numbering Todo.

### As-built ladder

- 0.69.2-0 docs: open TODO.md move cycle
- 0.69.2-1 docs: move notes/todo.md to TODO.md
- 0.69.2-2 refactor: TODO.md as validate/fix-todo default
- 0.69.2 docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md (close-out)

file.content:
## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bulleted list of the development "ladder":
   - 0.xx.y-0 blah (done)
   - 0.xx.y-1 blah blah (current)
   - 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - 0.xx.y close-out and validation

**docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md**

The todo list is the project's live state and the routine
acquaint read; root-level uppercase puts it in the
conventional root-file family (README, LICENSE, AGENTS.md,
ARCHITECTURE.md) — the same "easy for everyone to find"
argument that put AGENTS.md at the root. Siblings
(`todo-backlog.md`, `bugs.md`, `done.md`) stay in `notes/` —
TODO.md is the entry point, the README→docs/ shape.

- Decision (2026-07-16): shared AGENTS.md keeps hard paths
  (greppable) rather than naming the file abstractly.
- The AGENTS.md "File reads" section (and cycle-protocol.md's
  two mentions) are part of the shared byte-identical set, so
  this is a three-project change (vc-x1, vc-template-x1,
  iiac-perf) applied identically; vc-x1 goes first, breaking
  byte-identity until the other two apply the same change.
- Historical files (chores-NN.md, done.md, dated
  manifests/audits) keep their `notes/todo.md` mentions.

Plan:
- 0.69.2-0 docs: open TODO.md move cycle (done)
- 0.69.2-1 docs: move notes/todo.md to TODO.md (done)
  - `mv` + jj rename detection; TODO.md's relative links
    gain `notes/` prefixes; inbound links update (AGENTS.md,
    cycle-protocol.md, README.md, ARCHITECTURE.md,
    notes/README.md, bugs.md, refactor-20260716.md, live
    design notes).
- 0.69.2-2 refactor: TODO.md as validate/fix-todo default
  (done)
  - `TODO_FILE` const → `TODO.md` — the no-arg default must
    follow the move (a behavior change; the Todo entry's
    "code behavior is unaffected" held only for doc
    strings); src/ doc-string sweep + README default-path
    mentions.
- 0.69.2 docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md (close-out)

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,

- docs: shared protocol sync + jj refactor plan — adopted the vc-template-x1 shared notes set (AGENTS.md, cycle-protocol.md, versioning.md, jj-tips.md) with vc-x1's 0.69.0 corrections ratified template-side (manifest: [notes-sync-20260716.md](notes/notes-sync-20260716.md)); jj facade → jj-lib refactor program planned in [refactor-20260716.md](notes/refactor-20260716.md), absorbing eight Todos [[1]]

content.text:
0.69.2 close-out work is complete — ready for your review. The diff:

- **TODO.md** — the In Progress block removed (section back to its explainer); Done gains a one-line entry for the cycle with new ref `[2]` pointing at the chores section. Per the "retiring Done entries" sweep I kept all four prior Done entries — they're from this week and still nearby context for the refactor program.
- **notes/chores/chores-13.md** — the cycle section filled in: `Commits: [[33]],[[34]],[[35]]` backfilled (the three now-permanent cycle commits; the close-out's own SHA gets backfilled on a future push), narrative from the In Progress block plus two review-surfaced points (the `TODO_FILE` behavior-change correction, the untested wrapper layer), and an `### As-built ladder`. Refs [33]–[35] added.
- **Cargo.toml/lock** — `0.69.2` (bare).
- notes/README.md needed no functionality update (already handled in `-1`).

Validation (mandatory at close-out): fmt clean, clippy clean, all 5 test suites pass, installed — `vc-x1 -V` reports `0.69.2`, and no-arg `validate-todo` targets TODO.md.

On shape: the three cycle commits were pushed individually as they landed ("keep separate"), so they're immutable on `main` — the close-out pushes as a fourth separate commit, no squash/merge choice remains.

Ready to commit.

Here's the proposed description for the close-out — please review.

**Title:**

```
docs: move todo.md to root TODO.md
```

**Body:**

```
Close-out (0.69.2): bookkeeping — the cycle's record moves
to its permanent homes. Full cargo cycle run: fmt/clippy
clean, all suites pass, installed 0.69.2; no-arg
validate-todo targets TODO.md.

- TODO.md: In Progress block removed (explainer restored);
  Done entry for the cycle added with new ref [2]; prior
  Done entries kept (recent, nearby context)
- notes/chores/chores-13.md: cycle section filled — Commits:
  backfilled ([[33]],[[34]],[[35]]), narrative + two
  review-surfaced points (TODO_FILE behavior change,
  untested wrapper layer), As-built ladder; refs [33]-[35]
  added
- Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock: version 0.69.2
```

On your OK I'll run `vc-x1 push main` with this title/body.
